title: Aircraft Security Risk Assessment (SRA) coverage register - DO-326A Threat Conditions vs Cyber Security Items vs residual-risk acceptance with EASA / FAA finding-of-non-compliance early-warning
exclusions: Excludes ground-based projects governed by DO-391 / ED-205 (airline operations centres, airport systems) rather than DO-326A. Excludes paint / interior STCs explicitly out-of-scope for cybersecurity per the project PSecAA.
evidence: Saved search `UC-22.60.2` archived daily to `audit_evidence` with `do326a_clause="DO-326A-s3-3"`. Every row pins (project_id, airframe_type, csi_id, csi_name, tc_id, tc_safety_effect, sra_status, residual_risk, control_count, verification_evidence_ref, days_to_freeze, risk_score).

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §3.3 requires a Security Risk Assessment for every Threat Condition that combines likelihood and consequence to produce residual risk. This UC implements the per-project SRA coverage register and surfaces unmitigated residual risks that block the security architecture freeze milestone.
compliance.controlObjective: Demonstrates that the project's Threat Conditions list has been fully assessed in the SRA, that every CSI has a mapped risk-treatment status, and that residual risks exceeding the acceptable threshold are still under active treatment.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search archived to `audit_evidence`, the `DO-326A SRA Coverage and Residual Risk` dashboard, the project Security Risk Register, the verification-evidence index entries.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-326A §3.2 requires the project to identify Threat Conditions; the SRA presupposes the TC list is complete and current.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that the Threat Conditions list is the authoritative input to the SRA and is maintained through configuration management.

compliance.assurance_rationale: DO-356A §3.1 specifies the methods by which the SRA is performed; the SRA register evidence quality depends on consistent method application.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that the SRA method documented in the PSecAA is the method actually used for each Threat Condition assessment.

compliance.assurance_rationale: EASA Part-IS IS.OR.205 requires every approved organisation (including DOA holders) to perform an information-security risk assessment; the DO-326A SRA is the type-level realisation for design organisations.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidences that DOA-holder organisations satisfy the IS.OR.205 risk-assessment obligation through the type-project SRA.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Add a new Threat Condition to the TC register without an SRA entry against any CSI within 14 days of TC addition. UC-22.60.2 MUST flag with risk_score >= 80.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Complete an SRA entry for every CSI mapped to the new TC within 7 days, with residual_risk classified and either control_count > 0 or residual_risk_accepted_by populated. UC-22.60.2 MUST record `sra_status=complete` for the new TC and `risk_score=0`.

dataSources: KV Store `do326a_csi_lookup` (project_id, csi_id, csi_name, csi_type, criticality, applicable_tcs). KV Store `do326a_tcr_lookup` (project_id, tc_id, tc_description, tc_safety_effect, tc_classification). KV Store `do326a_sra_lookup` (project_id, csi_id, tc_id, likelihood, consequence, residual_risk, control_count, verification_evidence_ref, residual_risk_accepted_by, sra_status, sra_review_date). Project milestone register from `do326a_psecaa_lookup` (for arch_freeze due dates).
app: Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621).
spl:
| inputlookup do326a_tcr_lookup
| lookup do326a_csi_lookup project_id OUTPUT csi_id csi_name criticality applicable_tcs
| eval csi_id_list = split(applicable_tcs, ",")
| mvexpand csi_id_list
| where csi_id_list = tc_id
| lookup do326a_sra_lookup project_id csi_id tc_id OUTPUT sra_status residual_risk control_count verification_evidence_ref residual_risk_accepted_by sra_review_date
| lookup do326a_psecaa_lookup project_id milestone_id AS arch_freeze_milestone OUTPUT milestone_due_date AS arch_freeze_due
| eval now_ts = now()
| eval days_to_freeze = round((arch_freeze_due - now_ts) / 86400, 1)
| eval sra_status = case(
    isnull(sra_status), "missing",
    sra_status = "in-progress" AND residual_risk IN ("high", "hazardous", "catastrophic") AND isnull(residual_risk_accepted_by), "unmitigated",
    sra_status = "in-progress", "in-progress",
    sra_status = "complete", "complete",
    1=1, sra_status)
| eval risk_score = case(
    sra_status = "missing" AND days_to_freeze <= 30, 100,
    sra_status = "unmitigated" AND days_to_freeze <= 30, 95,
    sra_status = "missing" AND days_to_freeze <= 90, 85,
    sra_status = "unmitigated", 80,
    sra_status = "missing", 70,
    sra_status = "in-progress" AND days_to_freeze <= 14, 60,
    1=1, 0)
| where risk_score > 0
| sort 0 -risk_score, +days_to_freeze
| table project_id airframe_type csi_id csi_name tc_id tc_safety_effect sra_status residual_risk control_count verification_evidence_ref days_to_freeze risk_score
description: Joins the Threat Conditions register against the CSI inventory and the SRA register, surfaces missing SRA entries, in-progress SRAs against the security-architecture-freeze milestone, and unmitigated residual risks that have not been formally accepted. Designed to give the CISO a single-pane view of the security-architecture freeze gate.
value: Missing or unmitigated SRA entries are the single most common cause of FAA / EASA pre-finalisation findings that push a TC out of its target entry-into-service window. The SRA gap is also the basis on which an authority may withdraw or condition the TC after entry-into-service if a new threat condition emerges (e.g. the FAA Special Conditions for Boeing 787 added in 2007 reacted to a previously-unaddressed TC). This UC closes the gap on the project's own clock.
implementation: (1) Populate `do326a_csi_lookup` from the engineering bill-of-materials / configuration-management system; populate `do326a_tcr_lookup` from the project's Threat Conditions document; populate `do326a_sra_lookup` from the project's Security Risk Register (typically a controlled Excel or GRC workflow). (2) Schedule UC-22.60.2 daily at 07:00 programme-local; route `risk_score >= 80` to the project security focal + Chief Engineer + CISO at severity P1, `risk_score 40-79` at severity P2. (3) Build the `DO-326A SRA Coverage and Residual Risk` dashboard with per-project, per-CSI, and per-TC views.
visualization: (Panel 1) Open critical-risk SRA gaps sorted by risk_score. (Panel 2) SRA-completion burndown over time per project. (Panel 3) Residual-risk distribution (No-effect / Minor / Major / Hazardous / Catastrophic) per project. (Panel 4) Days-to-freeze countdown per project.

references.title: RTCA DO-326A - Airworthiness Security Process Specification
RTCA DO-356A - Airworthiness Security Methods and Considerations
EASA Acceptable Means of Compliance and Guidance Material

knownFalsePositives:
1. **TC added mid-project**: a new TC added in response to threat intelligence is briefly in `missing` SRA status; allow 14 days for analyst pickup. 2. **Residual risk accepted at programme-level**: some residual risks are accepted with management visibility (residual_risk_accepted_by populated); ensure the acceptance is captured. 3. **Joint SRA across projects**: a TC may be assessed at fleet level rather than per project; honour `joint_sra_reference` if populated.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `do326a_22_60_2_exceptions` with the Chief Engineer + CISO documented approval, an explicit expiry date, and a written justification.
reviewer: Aviation Cybersecurity SME Panel + Chief Engineer + Programme Director + CISO
detailedImplementation:
## Prerequisites

- UC-22.60.1 in production (gives the milestone register that drives `days_to_freeze`).
- KV Stores `do326a_csi_lookup`, `do326a_tcr_lookup`, `do326a_sra_lookup`.
- ServiceNow GRC or equivalent for Security Risk Register workflow.

## Step 1 - Security Risk Register KV Store

```ini
[do326a_sra_lookup]
enforceTypes = true
field.project_id = string
field.csi_id = string
field.tc_id = string
field.likelihood = string
field.consequence = string
field.residual_risk = string
field.control_count = number
field.verification_evidence_ref = string
field.residual_risk_accepted_by = string
field.sra_status = string
field.sra_review_date = time
```

## Step 2 - Joint SRA support

Where a TC is assessed once across a project family (e.g. the same engine family across multiple airframe types), populate `joint_sra_reference` in `do326a_sra_lookup` and exclude duplicated rows from the alert.

## Step 3 - Schedule

```ini
[UC-22.60.2: DO-326A SRA Coverage and Residual Risk]
cron_schedule = 0 7 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d@d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index, notable
action.summary_index._name = audit_evidence
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.60.2,reg=DO-326A,clause=DO-326A-s3-3
alert.severity = 5
```

## Step 4 - Runbooks

**`risk_score >= 80`** (owner: Chief Engineer + CISO, severity P1): Within 24 hours, the security focal schedules an SRA review; within 14 days, the SRA entry is brought to `complete` or the residual risk is formally accepted with sign-off documented.

**`risk_score 40-79`** (owner: Security Focal, severity P2): Within 10 business days, drive the SRA entry to closure.

## Step 5 - Aviation-specific notes

- **CS-25 / Part 25 vs CS-27 / CS-29**: large-aeroplane projects have richer SRA expectations than small-rotorcraft projects; calibrate the residual-risk threshold per certification basis.
- **DOA holder lineage**: a DOA holder's projects inherit organisational ISMS evidence; ensure `do326a_psecaa_lookup` carries the parent-organisation reference.
- **Issue Paper carry-over**: an Issue Paper or CRI may add TCs late in the project; ensure the TC list is regenerated when an IP/CRI is published.
grandmaExplanation: Engineers list all the ways a hacker might attack an aircraft and rate how dangerous each one is. We check that every risk has been studied and fixed, so the regulator can sign off and the aircraft can fly.
